concerns.text: With 5 datasets × 3 models = 15 observations, statistical power is severely limited for regression analysis with multiple predictors (base rate difference, feature dimensionality, class imbalance ratio). No formal power calculation quantifies detectable effect sizes. This undermines the ability to detect meaningful relationships between dataset characteristics and metric divergence.

Fixed-effects regression with only 5 datasets provides unstable variance estimates. While mixed-effects is correctly rejected (n<10), the fixed-effects approach with 5 groups may still produce unreliable coefficient estimates and confidence intervals. Consider reporting this as a study limitation with explicit effect size bounds.

SC-005 measures sample size adequacy against '≥15 observations' but doesn't validate whether 15 observations is statistically sufficient for regression stability with multiple predictors. A formal power analysis or minimum detectable effect size should be specified to justify the sample size.

FR-009 requires 'metric selection guidance output mapping dataset characteristics to recommended fairness metrics.' This language implies prescriptive recommendations that could be misinterpreted as causal. The associational-only framing (FR-008) should be explicitly reinforced in FR-009 to prevent causal misinterpretation.
